summary: Started moving GObj code from raw assembly into src/sysdolphin by adding src/sysdolphin/gobj.c for func_80390C5C and func_80390C84, while preserving matching output with inline asm under the matching path and keeping readable NON_MATCHING C versions. The PR also corrected an HSD_GObj field name from data_kind to user_data_kind based on assert evidence, added gobjuserdata.h with HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE, declared the new functions in gobj.h, and updated obj_files.mk so src/sysdolphin/gobj.o builds before the remaining asm/gobj.o.
what_changed: Added new source file src/sysdolphin/gobj.c containing func_80390C5C and func_80390C84.
Removed the corresponding func_80390C5C and func_80390C84 assembly blocks from asm/gobj.s.
For both functions, provided readable C implementations under #ifdef NON_MATCHING and exact inline asm implementations otherwise.
func_80390C5C iterates through gobj->proc child links, sets bit 0x80 in each HSD_GObjProc flags byte, and returns TRUE in the NON_MATCHING version.
func_80390C84 iterates through gobj->proc child links, clears bit 0x80 in each HSD_GObjProc flags byte, and returns FALSE in the NON_MATCHING version.
Added src/sysdolphin/gobj.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before asm/gobj.o so decompiled GObj code is linked while the remaining assembly file stays available.
Renamed HSD_GObj field data_kind to user_data_kind in src/sysdolphin/gobj.h, with the PR body stating this was based on an assert.
Added function prototypes for func_80390C5C and func_80390C84 to gobj.h.
Added src/sysdolphin/gobjuserdata.h with include guard _gobjuserdata_h_, dolphin types include, and HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE defined as -1.
smart_moves: Kept the project matching by moving only the relevant assembly routines into inline asm rather than forcing imperfect C to match immediately.
Documented intended behavior with NON_MATCHING C versions, giving future contributors a clear semantic target for eventually replacing the inline asm.
Used assert-derived naming evidence to improve struct field accuracy from generic data_kind to user_data_kind.
Split GObj user-data constants into a dedicated gobjuserdata.h header, mirroring the underlying subsystem instead of leaving the define ad hoc.
Updated obj_files.mk in a way that allows gradual source migration: new src/sysdolphin/gobj.o is added while the remaining asm/gobj.o continues to build.
decomp_lessons: When a function is understood but not yet matchable, a useful intermediate step is to add a NON_MATCHING C implementation plus a matching inline asm implementation in the new source file.
Moving routines out of asm/gobj.s should be paired with obj_files.mk ordering changes so the new source object is linked alongside any remaining assembly object.
Assert strings or assert references can provide strong evidence for struct field names and constants; this PR used that evidence for user_data_kind and HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE.
For process-list operations, HSD_GObjProc appears to be walked through gobj->proc and then p->child, with flags at byte offset 0x0D in the assembly corresponding to the flags field.
A decompiled source file can be introduced incrementally with only a small number of functions, rather than waiting for an entire asm file to be converted.
naming_conventions: Prefer HSD_GObj field name user_data_kind over data_kind when referring to the signed byte after obj_kind; the PR states this was assert-derived.
Use HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE for the no-user-data sentinel value, defined as -1.
Follow existing SysDolphin type prefixes such as HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc.
The added header uses the lowercase include guard form _gobjuserdata_h_, matching the file name rather than an all-caps project-wide style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed complete assembly functions from asm/gobj.s and reintroduced them as inline asm in src/sysdolphin/gobj.c under the matching path.
Used #ifdef NON_MATCHING to keep a readable C equivalent while preserving byte-matching code by default.
Inline asm bodies retained original addresses, instructions, and labels such as lbl_80390C78 and lbl_80390CA0 for traceability.
The matching asm uses nofralloc and preserves the original PowerPC control flow: load gobj->proc from offset 0x18, loop until NULL, update byte offset 0x0D, advance through offset 0x00.
For bit update matching, the assembly uses rlwimi to insert r3 into bit 7 of the flags byte; the NON_MATCHING C expresses this as setting or clearing 0x80.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.

The PR body explicitly notes three motivations: fixing a struct variable name according to an assert, adding gobjuserdata.h with an assert-derived define, and bringing inline asm into source for functions that were difficult to match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match func_80390C5C or func_80390C84 without inline asm.
Search for uses of user_data_kind and HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE to confirm the assert-derived naming propagated consistently.
Inspect later changes to gobjuserdata.h for additional user data constants or struct definitions.
Look for future refactors that replace _gobjuserdata_h_ include guard style or reorganize SysDolphin headers.
Search for other PRs that use NON_MATCHING C plus inline asm as a transitional matching tactic.
